summary: The prognosis of occupational allergic contact dermatitis improves significantly when the allergen is completely avoidable; however, continuous exposure leads to chronic recurrence and extended recovery times regardless of severity at onset. Thus, the presence—or absence—of consistent allergen exposure determines long-term outcomes.
